title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Some Reasons Why You Have to pay higher premium for life insurance
Introduction
If you're just starting to look for life insurance plans, you may be wondering how much money you'll have to spend for the coverage. There are several criteria that go into determining the premium you pay, and it might be difficult to understand why you must pay a greater rate for life insurance than others.
Top  reasons for paying higher premium for life insurance
Age
Your insurance premium will increase as you become older. You are less prone to have health problems while you are young, and even if you do, you are more likely to recover quicker thanks to a stronger immune system. As a result, it is usually preferable to get insurance plans while you are young rather than later in life.
Gender
In comparison to females, males are also more prone to smoke, drink alcohol, or use cigarettes1. As a result, women are more inclined to pay a lower premium than their male counterparts.
Previous medical history
Before issuing you a policy, most of the  insurance companies require you to undergo a comprehensive health examination. They will also go through your medical records to see whether you have any pre-existing problems. You may be required to pay a higher premium if you have chronic diseases. This is since persons with health problems are more prone to file insurance claims. Before providing you an insurance policy, your insurer will analyze the risk. If you've had any operations or treatments in the past, you'll need to tell your insurer  about them. Before your coverage takes effect, certain insurance providers may require you to serve a waiting period.
Physical fitness
You are more likely to develop lifestyle problems if you are overweight or live a sedentary lifestyle.. Obesity is defined as a body mass index of 30 or above. Obese or unfit people pay a substantially  more premium than those with a normal BMI for their age and height.
Medical history in the family
Another important aspect in determining how much premium the insurance company will want you to pay is your family's medical history. Assume a member in your immediate family suffers from genetic conditions. In such a situation, you are at a greater risk of contracting the disease. As a result, depending on your family's medical history, you may be requested to pay a higher premium since the chances of filing a claim are greater.
Hobbies
Skydiving and other activities as a profession require a substantial level of risk and might result in higher premium amounts. This is because you are often exposed to hazardous activities that might harm you and, as a result, enhance your chances of filing an insurance claim.
Tobacco usage and smoking
It is common knowledge that smoking or using tobacco increases your chances of heart attacks and cancer. As a result, life insurance premiums for smokers and tobacco users are much higher than for non-smokers and non-tobacco users. If you smoke or use tobacco, you must tell your insurance company right away! Whether you currently have an insurance policy with a smoker status and haven't smoked in a while, ask your insurance provider if changing your status from "smoker" to "nonsmoker" would affect your premium rate.
Excessive alcohol consumption
If you drink alcohol on a regular basis, your insurance may charge you a higher premium. This is because drinking too much alcohol puts you at a greater risk of developing liver problems and other illnesses. Whether your existing insurance policy classifies you as an alcoholic and you have completely stopped drinking, you may  check with your insurer to see if this change impacts your premium payment rate.
Wrapping Up
Today, your life insurance premium may seem to be a payment, like your mortgage. However, if your family confronts financial hardship because of your untimely death, the premiums you pay will be more than simply another monthly payment. In a nutshell, your life insurance plans will provide financial security for your loved ones.
Note: Remember that this is not an exhaustive list, and there may be more reasons for paying higher premium for life insurance depending on the insurance plan and their underwriting.
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
What is the purpose of life insurance?
Life insurance may help safeguard your family's financial security in your absence. In the event of death, the insurance also pays the benefits to your nominees. Obtaining such coverage assures that your family can fulfill their financial obligations and maintain their lives even if you are not present.
What is the cost of life insurance?
The cost of insurance is determined by the kind of coverage selected. In addition, the cost of insurance is influenced by various criteria. You must study the policy paperwork to learn all there is to know about life insurance prices.
Source:
1http://rchiips.org/nfhs/NFHS-5Reports/NFHS-5_INDIA_REPORT.pdf (refer to page 62)
https://www.livemint.com/insurance/news/factors-that-may-hike-your-insurance-premium-1562685413162.html
https://www.financialexpress.com/money/why-premium-on-life-insurance-plans-varies-across-companies/2375837/
